system: You are proposing baseline QUALITY GATES for a software system, given a list of its observable signals. A gate is a metric over one or more signals that is worth monitoring. Do NOT set any threshold/target number (a human will). For each gate give a compute type from [rate, ratio, p95, psi, count, mean], a direction ('min' = higher-is-better, 'max' = lower-is-better), and 'inputs' = the list of signal keys it is computed from. Return JSON: {"gates":[{"key","description","direction","compute","inputs","segment_dimension","rationale"}]}.
raw_response: {"gates":[{"key":"availability.healthcheck_success_rate","description":"Proportion of healthcheck requests that return 200 OK (service availability signal).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.healthcheck.200"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance","rationale":"Tracks basic service availability and routing; a drop indicates outages, misconfig, or dependency failures."},{"key":"todos.read_success_rate","description":"Proportion of todo read requests that succeed (list + get 200) relative to all read outcomes (including expected 404s).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.list.200","http.todos.get.200","http.todos.get.404"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,route","rationale":"Measures end-user read experience while accounting for expected not-found behavior; shifts can indicate data access issues or routing problems."},{"key":"todos.get_not_found_ratio","description":"Share of GET-by-id requests resulting in 404 (not found) relative to all GET-by-id outcomes.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.get.404","http.todos.get.200"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,client_id","rationale":"Detects client-side ID misuse, stale caches, or data loss; sudden increases can indicate persistence or replication issues."},{"key":"todos.create_success_rate","description":"Proportion of create requests that succeed (201) relative to create outcomes (including validation failures).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.create.201","http.todos.create.400.missing_title","http.server.500.global_error"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,client_id","rationale":"Captures create reliability and validation behavior; separates user input issues from server faults via inclusion of 500s."},{"key":"todos.create_validation_error_ratio","description":"Share of create attempts failing due to missing/blank title relative to create successes + this validation error.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.create.400.missing_title","http.todos.create.201"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,client_id","rationale":"Monitors client/API contract adherence and UX regressions (e.g., frontend not sending title)."},{"key":"todos.update_success_rate","description":"Proportion of update requests that succeed (200) relative to update outcomes (including expected 400/404 and 500).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.update.200","http.todos.update.400.no_fields","http.todos.update.404","http.server.500.global_error"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,client_id","rationale":"Measures update reliability while accounting for expected client and not-found errors; highlights server-side regressions when success rate drops."},{"key":"todos.update_no_fields_ratio","description":"Share of update requests rejected for having no updatable fields relative to update successes + this validation error.","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.update.400.no_fields","http.todos.update.200"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,client_id","rationale":"Detects client bugs or API misuse (sending empty bodies/unsupported fields) and helps validate request-shaping changes."},{"key":"todos.update_not_found_ratio","description":"Share of update requests resulting in 404 relative to update outcomes (200 + 404).","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.update.404","http.todos.update.200"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,client_id","rationale":"Surfaces stale IDs, race conditions, or data consistency issues affecting updates."},{"key":"todos.delete_success_rate","description":"Proportion of delete requests that succeed (204) relative to delete outcomes (204 + 404 + 500).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.delete.204","http.todos.delete.404","http.server.500.global_error"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,client_id","rationale":"Tracks delete reliability and distinguishes expected not-found from server failures."},{"key":"todos.delete_not_found_ratio","description":"Share of delete requests resulting in 404 relative to delete outcomes (204 + 404).","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.delete.404","http.todos.delete.204"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,client_id","rationale":"Detects client-side stale IDs and potential data loss; spikes can indicate unexpected deletions or inconsistent state."},{"key":"server_global_error_rate","description":"Rate of unhandled server errors (500 global error) across all traffic.","direction":"max","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.server.500.global_error"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance,route","rationale":"Primary stability signal; increases indicate regressions, dependency failures, or unhandled edge cases."},{"key":"startup_log_emission_count","description":"Count of backend startup log messages (used to detect restarts and crash loops).","direction":"max","compute":"count","inputs":["log.backend.startup"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance","rationale":"Unexpected increases indicate frequent restarts; absence can indicate logging/boot failures."},{"key":"port_discovery_write_success_count","description":"Count of successful writes of backend port discovery file for frontend discovery.","direction":"min","compute":"count","inputs":["file.backend_port.discovery"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance","rationale":"Ensures the backend publishes its port for dependent components; missing events can break frontend-backend connectivity."},{"key":"port_discovery_coverage_ratio","description":"Ratio of port discovery file writes to backend startups (coverage of discovery mechanism per boot).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["file.backend_port.discovery","log.backend.startup"],"segment_dimension":"env,region,instance","rationale":"Validates that each startup results in a discoverable port; catches partial initialization and filesystem permission issues."}]}

gates.description: Proportion of healthcheck requests that return 200 OK (service availability signal).
gates.rationale: Tracks basic service availability and routing; a drop indicates outages, misconfig, or dependency failures.

gates.description: Proportion of todo read requests that succeed (list + get 200) relative to all read outcomes (including expected 404s).
gates.rationale: Measures end-user read experience while accounting for expected not-found behavior; shifts can indicate data access issues or routing problems.

gates.description: Share of GET-by-id requests resulting in 404 (not found) relative to all GET-by-id outcomes.
gates.rationale: Detects client-side ID misuse, stale caches, or data loss; sudden increases can indicate persistence or replication issues.

gates.description: Proportion of create requests that succeed (201) relative to create outcomes (including validation failures).
gates.rationale: Captures create reliability and validation behavior; separates user input issues from server faults via inclusion of 500s.

gates.description: Share of create attempts failing due to missing/blank title relative to create successes + this validation error.
gates.rationale: Monitors client/API contract adherence and UX regressions (e.g., frontend not sending title).

gates.description: Proportion of update requests that succeed (200) relative to update outcomes (including expected 400/404 and 500).
gates.rationale: Measures update reliability while accounting for expected client and not-found errors; highlights server-side regressions when success rate drops.

gates.description: Share of update requests rejected for having no updatable fields relative to update successes + this validation error.
gates.rationale: Detects client bugs or API misuse (sending empty bodies/unsupported fields) and helps validate request-shaping changes.

gates.description: Share of update requests resulting in 404 relative to update outcomes (200 + 404).
gates.rationale: Surfaces stale IDs, race conditions, or data consistency issues affecting updates.

gates.description: Proportion of delete requests that succeed (204) relative to delete outcomes (204 + 404 + 500).
gates.rationale: Tracks delete reliability and distinguishes expected not-found from server failures.

gates.description: Share of delete requests resulting in 404 relative to delete outcomes (204 + 404).
gates.rationale: Detects client-side stale IDs and potential data loss; spikes can indicate unexpected deletions or inconsistent state.

gates.description: Rate of unhandled server errors (500 global error) across all traffic.
gates.rationale: Primary stability signal; increases indicate regressions, dependency failures, or unhandled edge cases.

gates.description: Count of backend startup log messages (used to detect restarts and crash loops).
gates.rationale: Unexpected increases indicate frequent restarts; absence can indicate logging/boot failures.

gates.description: Count of successful writes of backend port discovery file for frontend discovery.
gates.rationale: Ensures the backend publishes its port for dependent components; missing events can break frontend-backend connectivity.

gates.description: Ratio of port discovery file writes to backend startups (coverage of discovery mechanism per boot).
gates.rationale: Validates that each startup results in a discoverable port; catches partial initialization and filesystem permission issues.
